data.title: Chesapeake Utilities Non-GAAP EPS of $2.22 misses by $0.03, revenue of $298.7M beats by $50.25M
data.description:
• Chesapeake Utilities press release (NYSE:CPK): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.22 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $298.7M (+21.6% Y/Y) beats by $50.25M.
  • Shares -0.41% AH.

  • The Company continues to re-affirm its 2025 EPS guidance range of $6.15 to $6.35 per share, as well as the 2028 EPS guidance range of $7.75 to $8.00 per share. The 2028 guidance implies an annual EPS growth rate of approximately 8 percent from the 2025 EPS guidance, or since 2018, an 8.5 percent growth rate.

    These earnings projections are based upon the Company's previously announced capital expenditure guidance for the five-year period ended 2028 of $1.5 billion to $1.8 billion. The Company continues to re-affirm this five-year capital guidance and projects capital expenditures of $325 million to $375 million for 2025.

  • FY EPS consensus of $6.24

data.title: Delek outlines $120M cash flow improvement through EOP in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Delek US Holdings, Inc. (DK) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Avigal Soreq highlighted progress in improving operational performance despite a challenging refining margin environment. He emphasized the completion of planned plant outages at the Tyler and Big Spring refineries and the ongoing advancements in enterprise optimization plans (EOP). The company remains focused on achieving $120 million in annual cash flow improvements starting in the second half of 2025.
  • The deconsolidation of Delek Logistics (DKL) was underscored as a key initiative. Recent inter-company transactions increased third-party cash flow at DKL to approximately 80% and improved liquidity at DK by $250 million. These moves aim to ensure the value of $400 million in third-party EBITDA at DKL is reflected in DK’s share price.
  • CFO Mark Hobbs reported a net loss of $173 million for the quarter, with adjusted EBITDA of $26.5 million. He also noted a $42.2 million increase in refining EBITDA due to higher margins and throughput, and $117 million in adjusted EBITDA from the logistics segment.

Outlook

  • The company reiterated its guidance for DKL’s 2025 EBITDA, projected between $480 million and $520 million. DKL’s growth is supported by its prime location in the Permian Basin and advancements in gas processing capabilities, including the Libby 2 gas plant expansion.
  • For Q2 2025, throughput guidance includes 73,000–77,000 barrels per day at Tyler, 80,000–84,000 barrels per day at El Dorado, and a system-wide throughput target of 302,000–318,000 barrels per day. Operating expenses are expected to range between $215 million and $225 million.
  • Guidance reflects ongoing cost controls and operational improvements, with projected gains in free cash flow through EOP initiatives.

Financial Results

  • Losses in supply and marketing narrowed to $23.7 million in Q1 2025 compared to $34.6 million in Q4 2024, driven by seasonal trends and structural improvements.
  • Refining throughput and margins showed mixed performance across facilities. Tyler achieved a production margin of $7.82 per barrel, Big Spring recorded $4.86 per barrel, and El Dorado improved to $3.83 per barrel. Krotz Springs reached record throughput of 85,000 barrels per day, with a margin of $6.40 per barrel.
  • Cash flow from operations reflected a use of $62 million, including timing-related working capital movements and investments in growth projects like the Libby 2 gas plant.

Q&A

  • Alexa Patrick, Goldman Sachs, inquired about capital returns strategy. CEO Soreq reiterated the focus on free cash flow and described a balanced approach between dividends, share repurchases, and deleveraging. He noted, “We see a huge amount of value in our share price.”
  • Matthew Blair, TPH, asked about supply and marketing dynamics. Management attributed Q1 losses to seasonality but expressed optimism for Q2, citing stronger demand and crack spreads.
  • Manav Gupta, UBS, questioned the scope of small refinery exemptions (SREs). Management confirmed efforts to secure retroactive exemptions back to 2019 and anticipated forward-looking relief as well.
  • McKinley Trusclair, Wolfe Research, explored upside potential beyond the $120 million EOP target. CEO Soreq affirmed possibilities for exceeding the guidance, citing continued operational improvements.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with questions focusing on capital allocation, operational improvements, and regulatory outcomes. Concerns over seasonality and refining margins persisted.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing progress in operational metrics and EOP targets. The proactive addressing of challenges and focus on investor returns were recurring themes.
  • Compared to Q4 2024, management's tone was more optimistic, reflecting progress in refining operations and financial improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Refining throughput and margins improved sequentially, with system-wide throughput increasing from a Q4 2024 target of 278,000–292,000 barrels per day to 302,000–318,000 barrels per day in Q1 2025.
  • Supply and marketing losses narrowed significantly, indicating seasonal recovery and structural gains.
  • Management's confidence in achieving the upper end of EOP cash flow improvement guidance strengthened, marking a shift from prior cautious optimism.
  • Analysts maintained a consistent focus on operational cost controls and capital allocation strategies, mirroring prior quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the ongoing challenges of a volatile refining margin environment and seasonal demand fluctuations.
  • Analysts raised concerns about sustained weaknesses in inland markets and potential delays in regulatory outcomes, including SRE petitions.
  • Mitigation strategies include operational improvements, cost controls, and advances in EOP initiatives to enhance cash flow resiliency.

Final Takeaway

Delek US Holdings emphasized its strategic focus on operational improvements, enterprise optimization, and deconsolidation of Delek Logistics to unlock shareholder value. With an ambitious $120 million cash flow improvement target and strong throughput outlook for Q2 2025, the company appears well-positioned to navigate market challenges and capitalize on growth opportunities in the Permian Basin.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: FDA and OpenAI meeting to discuss AI in drug evaluation - Wired
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is holding talks with OpenAI, the Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) backed generative artificial intelligence startup, to discuss the agency’s use of AI, according to Wired citing sources.

The sources, who are close to the project, say a small team from OpenAI has met with the FDA and two associates of Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency multiple times in recent weeks.

The report also mentioned that the group has discussed a project called cderGPT, or Center for Drug Evaluation, which regulates over-the-counter and prescription drugs in the U.S.

Jeremy Walsh, who was recently named as the FDA’s first-ever AI officer, lead the discussions. 

Possibly hinting at this agreement, FDA commissioner Marty Makary posted on X on Wednesday, “Why does it take over 10 years for a new drug to come to market? Why are we not modernized with AI and other things? We’ve just completed our first AI-assisted scientific review for a product, and that’s just the beginning.” 

Wired mentioned in its report that no contract has been signed yet.

data.title: Schrödinger projects strong 2025 revenue growth with $45M-$50M in drug discovery
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Schrödinger, Inc. (SDGR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ramy Farid highlighted strong Q1 performance, reporting total revenue of $59.6 million, driven by $48.8 million in software revenue with 46% growth and $10.7 million in drug discovery revenue. Farid reiterated confidence in the full-year 2025 guidance and emphasized the continued adoption of their computational drug discovery platform.
  • Farid announced advancements in predictive toxicology solutions, with over 50 off-targets structurally enabled, and plans to release a beta version later this year. New capabilities were also introduced in protein degrader modeling, T-cell receptor structure prediction, and crystal structure prediction for drug formulation.
  • CFO Geoff Porges stated that operating expenses declined year-over-year, and the cash position improved significantly to $512 million, supported by collections from contracts and an upfront payment from Novartis.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated full-year 2025 guidance, projecting software revenue growth of 10%-15% and drug discovery revenue in the range of $45 million to $50 million. Software gross margins are expected to remain between 74%-75%.
  • For Q2 2025, software revenue is forecasted to range between $38 million and $42 million, with drug discovery revenue expected to be distributed evenly over the remaining quarters.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $59.6 million, up 63% year-over-year, with software revenue contributing $48.8 million and drug discovery revenue $10.7 million. These results included higher recognition of revenue from collaborations, particularly with Novartis.
  • Software gross margin was 72%, impacted by the Gates Foundation-funded Predictive Tox Initiative. Overall gross margin remained stable at 52%, consistent with Q1 2024.
  • Net loss for the quarter was $60 million, or $0.82 per share, reflecting higher total expenses and a loss in the fair value of equity method investments.

Q&A

  • Michael Yee, Jefferies: Asked about expectations for SGR-1505 data. Karen Akinsanya responded that the initial update will describe safety, pharmacokinetics, and preliminary efficacy data from the Phase 1 trial.
  • Brendan Smith, TD Cowen: Inquired about the predictive tox initiative. CEO Farid emphasized the differentiation of their solution, which integrates physics and machine learning for higher accuracy, and noted plans to determine pricing after beta testing.
  • Mani Foroohar, Leerink Partners: Questioned competition and AI threats. Farid explained the necessity of training sets for machine learning and assured that the company’s physics-based methods remain robust against potential disruptions.
  • Vikram Purohit, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the potential for partnering SGR-1505 post-data release. Akinsanya stated that Phase 1 data would guide further partnering discussions later this year.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts primarily focused on the clinical pipeline, financial guidance, and software offerings. The tone was generally positive, reflecting confidence in the company's progress and strategic direction.
  • Management maintained an optimistic tone, emphasizing strong financial results and advancements in technology. However, caution was expressed regarding the macroeconomic environment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed consistent confidence, with an added focus on the upcoming clinical data and predictive toxicology solutions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased significantly in Q1 2025 compared to Q4 2024, driven by strong software and drug discovery performance.
  • Management reiterated unchanged guidance for 2025, consistent with the previous quarter's outlook.
  • Strategic focus shifted slightly towards highlighting predictive toxicology and upcoming clinical trial data, reflecting progress in these areas.
  • Analysts continued to focus on financial sustainability and the impact of new product offerings, similar to concerns raised in Q4 2024.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted potential impacts from macroeconomic challenges and political uncertainties but stated that the business remains relatively protected.
  • Analyst concerns centered around the scalability of the predictive tox initiative and the ability to sustain revenue growth in a competitive landscape.
  • Management emphasized the importance of their cash position and technology differentiation as mitigating factors.

Final Takeaway

Schrödinger demonstrated a strong start to 2025, with notable revenue growth and advancements in software and drug discovery. The company reiterated its full-year guidance and highlighted progress in predictive toxicology and clinical programs, positioning it for continued growth and innovation. Upcoming clinical data releases and the beta launch of the predictive tox solution are expected to be key catalysts for the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
